summary: The additive guarded Run contract is regression-contained at HEAD 41a6cb0: legacy execute status remains on its original route, managed/repository/source-fallback authority and mirror paths fail closed, predecessor and corrupt-state handling are explicit, and current-head targeted plus full-suite evidence covers adjacent VibePro behavior.

inspection.summary: Inspected the complete origin/main...HEAD surface, Story/Architecture/test plan, CLI delegation, Git identity and Run persistence/transition code, regression-focused tests and current-head evidence; also replayed five compatibility and nullable-state tests (5/5 pass) without rerunning the full suite.
inputs: git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD (8 files; additive guarded Run module plus bounded CLI/Git identity changes)

node --test --test-name-pattern='recoverable transitions require|nullable state unions reject|legacy status without run-id|repair-linked-copy is rejected|unknown execute subcommands|historical stopped null' test/guarded-run-session.test.js (5/5 pass)

judgment_delta: Initial regression concern: adding execute run/watch/resume/cancel and shared option parsing could divert the existing execute status/start/next/reconcile/merge routes -> final: CLI dispatch enters the new service only for the four additive commands or status with an explicit --run-id; the focused legacy-status test proves status without --run-id still returns the legacy artifact, and the current-head full suite reports 1130/1130.

Initial path-surface concern: a happy-path Run implementation could silently trust mirrors, unavailable worktrees, malformed candidates, or legacy predecessor state -> final: authority selection, source-fallback fingerprinting, all-candidate validation, mirror divergence, explicit repair, migration, corrupt quarantine and future-schema stops are separate fail-closed paths with artifact-bearing errors and dedicated tests; suppression is not silent.

Initial compatibility concern: requiring typed stop metadata could make historical stopped artifacts unreadable or permit reason inheritance on new transitions -> final: new recoverable transitions require a fresh valid reason before authority persistence, canonical and 0.0.0/unversioned invalid unions are non-mutating invalid_state, while the explicitly documented historical null stop remains readable; focused replay passed.

Initial evidence concern: new-contract tests alone would not establish regression safety -> final: evidence is bound to the exact reviewed HEAD and combines 40/40 guarded contract scenarios with 1130/1130 repository-wide tests; no modified DB/UI/API surface or migration outside additive .vibepro Run artifacts was found.

managed_worktree_context.reason: review record is running inside the recorded managed worktree

content_binding.reason: strict HEAD binding was requested for this evidence
